title.en: SWE-Review: Closing the Loop on Issue Resolution with Agentic Code Review
title.zh: SWE-Review：通过智能代理代码审查实现问题解决的闭环

abstract: Coding agents increasingly generate pull requests (PRs) for real-world software issues, yet one-shot PR generation remains open-loop: the PR is proposed without systematic review, diagnosis, or revision. We introduce \textbf{SWE-Review}, a framework for closing this loop with agentic code review. Given an issue and an AI-generated PR, a reviewer agent explores the repository, decides whether the PR should be accepted, and provides structured feedback for revision. We evaluate this setting with our proposed \textbf{SWE-Review-Bench} to measure both review correctness and downstream revision usefulness. We further curate \textbf{SWE-Review-Traj} dataset to study broader applications of agentic review and fill the data-scarcity gap for open reviewer training. Experiments show that agentic review continuously improves PRs through a generate-review-revise loop, outperforms single-turn fixed-context review in both decision accuracy and resolve rate after revision, transfers beyond review to improve issue-resolution models, and enables effective and efficient test-time scaling. These results position agentic code review as a practical mechanism for moving AI coding agents from one-shot PR generation toward closed-loop issue resolution.

relevance.en: This paper extends your interest in avoiding overfitting by using agentic code review to iteratively detect and fix plausible but incorrect patches, with validation on SWE-bench Verified—a key APR benchmark—thus providing a stronger patch validation mechanism.
relevance.zh: 本文通过使用智能代理代码审查迭代地检测和修复看似合理但不正确的补丁，扩展了您对避免过拟合的兴趣，并在SWE-bench Verified——一个关键的APR基准——上进行验证，从而提供了一个更强的补丁验证机制。

figures.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
SWE-Review turns one-shot PR generation into closed-loop issue resolution. AI cod-
ing agents generate candidate PRs from real software issues; an agentic reviewer then inspects the
repository, issue, and candidate PR in an executable environment before emitting a binary decision
and structured diagnosis. Approved PRs are accepted as resolved, while rejected PRs are returned to
the coding agent for review-guided revision. On SWE-bench Verified, this closed loop continuously
raises the resolve rate across three PR generators, showing that agentic review is useful not only for
judging PRs but also for improving them.
caption.zh: SWE-Review将一次性PR生成转变为闭环问题解决。AI编码代理从真实软件问题生成候选PR；然后，智能代理审查员在可执行环境中检查仓库、问题和候选PR，之后发出二元决策和结构化诊断。批准的PR被视为已解决，而被拒绝的PR返回给编码代理进行审查指导的修订。在SWE-bench Verified上，这个闭环持续提高了三个PR生成器的解决率，表明智能代理审查不仅有助于判断PR，也有助于改进它们。

figures.reference_text:
• Agentic reviews continually improve PRs through the iterative loop. The generate-review- revise loop repeatedly improves candidate PRs on SWE-bench Verified, raising resolve rate from 27.5% to 56.9% for Qwen3-30B-A3B, from 50.9% to 68.8% for Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B, and from 72.2% to 75.4% for GLM-5 (Figure 1).

We formalize agentic code review as a repository-grounded task. For each review instance, the input consists of three parts: a repository checkout at the relevant commit, a natural-language issue, and a candidate PR with its proposed diff, title, and body. The reviewer does not receive the golden patch or hidden test results. It may browse files, search code, inspect dependencies, and execute commands in the repository environment before submitting a review report. The review report contains two fields. The decision is binary: approve the PR if the patch resolves the issue, or request changes otherwise. When requesting changes, the reviewer also provides a diagnosis that identifies concrete defects, cites relevant code locations when possible, and proposes actionable fixes for a downstream revision agent. In this formulation, review serves as the pivot of the generate-review-revise loop shown in Figure 1.

figures.label: Figure 2

caption.en:
Agentic review traces the bug across modules to its upstream root cause; single-turn
approves a downstream symptom fix that silently returns wrong results. On sympy-13877, the
candidate PR adds a NaN guard in exprtools.py that prevents the crash but still leaves det()
returning incorrect nan values. The agentic reviewer explores the repository, follows the call chain
to matrices.py, identifies the discarded result of cancel(ret), and requests changes with an
upstream fix suggestion. Full trajectory in Appendix D.
caption.zh: 智能代理审查跨模块追踪错误到其上游根本原因；单轮审查批准了一个下游症状修复，该修复静默返回了错误结果。在sympy-13877上，候选PR在exprtools.py中添加了一个NaN防护，防止了崩溃，但仍然导致det()返回不正确的nan值。智能代理审查员探索仓库，跟踪调用链到matrices.py，识别出cancel(ret)被丢弃的结果，并请求更改并附带上游修复建议。完整轨迹见附录D。

figures.reference_text: Single-turn review fixes the context before the reviewer knows what evidence is needed. This makes it brittle on non-local bugs: a PR can fix a symptom while leaving the root cause intact, depend on behavior outside the changed files, or violate conventions that are documented elsewhere. Agentic review instead gives the model an evidence-gathering procedure. It can inspect the repository, follow call chains, compare related implementations, run lightweight checks, and only then commit to a decision. Figure 2 illustrates this failure mode using an issue sympy-13877. The candidate PR adds a NaN guard that suppresses a crash, but the function still returns an incorrect nan. Single- turn reviewers approve the symptom fix. Agentic reviewers trace execution upstream, find that cancel(ret) is called without assigning its result, and reject the PR with the one-line upstream fix.

figure.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
SWE-Review turns one-shot PR generation into closed-loop issue resolution. AI cod-
ing agents generate candidate PRs from real software issues; an agentic reviewer then inspects the
repository, issue, and candidate PR in an executable environment before emitting a binary decision
and structured diagnosis. Approved PRs are accepted as resolved, while rejected PRs are returned to
the coding agent for review-guided revision. On SWE-bench Verified, this closed loop continuously
raises the resolve rate across three PR generators, showing that agentic review is useful not only for
judging PRs but also for improving them.
caption.zh: SWE-Review将一次性PR生成转变为闭环问题解决。AI编码代理从真实软件问题生成候选PR；然后，智能代理审查员在可执行环境中检查仓库、问题和候选PR，之后发出二元决策和结构化诊断。批准的PR被视为已解决，而被拒绝的PR返回给编码代理进行审查指导的修订。在SWE-bench Verified上，这个闭环持续提高了三个PR生成器的解决率，表明智能代理审查不仅有助于判断PR，也有助于改进它们。

figure.reference_text:
• Agentic reviews continually improve PRs through the iterative loop. The generate-review- revise loop repeatedly improves candidate PRs on SWE-bench Verified, raising resolve rate from 27.5% to 56.9% for Qwen3-30B-A3B, from 50.9% to 68.8% for Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B, and from 72.2% to 75.4% for GLM-5 (Figure 1).

We formalize agentic code review as a repository-grounded task. For each review instance, the input consists of three parts: a repository checkout at the relevant commit, a natural-language issue, and a candidate PR with its proposed diff, title, and body. The reviewer does not receive the golden patch or hidden test results. It may browse files, search code, inspect dependencies, and execute commands in the repository environment before submitting a review report. The review report contains two fields. The decision is binary: approve the PR if the patch resolves the issue, or request changes otherwise. When requesting changes, the reviewer also provides a diagnosis that identifies concrete defects, cites relevant code locations when possible, and proposes actionable fixes for a downstream revision agent. In this formulation, review serves as the pivot of the generate-review-revise loop shown in Figure 1.

brief_summary.en:
Current AI coding agents generate pull requests (PRs) in a single shot without systematic review, often fixing symptoms rather than root causes and leaving bugs unaddressed. To close this open-loop process, the authors introduce SWE-Review, a framework where a reviewer agent actively explores the repository—reading files, following call chains, and inspecting tests—before deciding whether to accept the PR and providing structured, localized feedback for revision.

This iterative generate-review-revise loop continuously improves PR quality: on SWE-Review-Bench, agentic review outperforms single-turn fixed-context review on both decision accuracy (e.g., ~75% vs. ~65%) and resolve rate after revision (~56% vs. ~45%). By detecting and correcting plausible but incorrect patches, the loop reduces overfitting and raises resolve rates on SWE-bench Verified by up to 29 percentage points. It also enables efficient test-time scaling, achieving stronger resolve than best-of-N sampling while using far fewer samples. These results position agentic code review as a practical mechanism for moving AI coding agents toward reliable, closed-loop issue resolution.
brief_summary.zh:
当前的AI编码代理以一次性方式生成拉取请求（PR），而没有系统性的审查，常常修复症状而非根本原因，导致错误未被解决。为了闭环这一开环过程，作者引入了SWE-Review，一个框架，其中审查代理主动探索仓库——读取文件、跟踪调用链、检查测试——然后再决定是否接受PR，并提供结构化的、局部的反馈以进行修订。

这种迭代的生成-审查-修订循环持续提升PR质量：在SWE-Review-Bench上，智能代理审查在决策准确性（例如，~75%对~65%）和修订后的解决率（~56%对~45%）上都优于单轮固定上下文审查。通过检测和纠正看似合理但不正确的补丁，该循环减少了过拟合，并在SWE-bench Verified上将解决率提升了最多29个百分点。它还实现了高效的测试时扩展，在远少于样本的情况下达到比最佳N采样更强的解决效果。这些结果将智能代理代码审查定位为一种实用机制，推动AI编码代理向可靠的、闭环的问题解决迈进。

problem.en: Current AI coding agents generate pull requests (PRs) in an open-loop, one-shot manner without systematic review, diagnosis, or revision. Once a PR is proposed, there is no structured process to verify its correctness, completeness, or adherence to project conventions. This leads to PRs that may contain bugs, incomplete fixes, or root causes not fully addressed, requiring significant human oversight for integration. Prior work has focused on improving PR generation but has largely ignored the review and revision stage, leaving a critical gap in closed-loop issue resolution. The paper illustrates this with concrete examples where a naïve fix addresses a symptom but leaves an upstream root cause untouched, resulting in incorrect behavior (e.g., returning wrong values instead of crashing). Thus, the lack of iterative, evidence-based code review is a major bottleneck for reliable autonomous issue resolution in real-world software engineering.
problem.zh: 当前的AI编码代理以一种开环、一次性方式生成拉取请求（PR），没有系统性的审查、诊断或修订。一旦提出PR，就没有结构化的过程来验证其正确性、完整性或是否符合项目约定。这导致PR可能包含错误、不完整的修复或未完全解决的根原因，需要大量的人工监督才能集成。先前的工作集中在改进PR生成上，但很大程度上忽略了审查和修订阶段，在闭环问题解决中留下了一个关键空白。本文通过具体例子说明，一个天真的修复解决了症状但未触及上游根本原因，导致不正确行为（例如，返回错误值而不是崩溃）。因此，缺乏迭代的、基于证据的代码审查是现实世界软件工程中可靠自主问题解决的主要瓶颈。

solution.en: SWE-Review is a framework that closes the loop on issue resolution by introducing agentic code review. Given an issue description and an AI-generated PR, a reviewer agent actively explores the repository—reading files, following call chains, inspecting test results—in an executable environment before making a binary decision (approve or request changes) and producing structured, localized feedback for revision. This differs fundamentally from single-turn fixed-context review, which uses only a static diff or surrounding code and is brittle for non-local bugs that span multiple files or modules. The key insight is that an agentic reviewer can adapt its information-gathering process based on evidence it uncovers, enabling it to trace symptoms to upstream root causes. This yields more accurate decisions and more actionable feedback, enabling a generate-review-revise loop that iteratively improves PR quality until the reviewer approves, thereby moving from open-loop generation to closed-loop resolution.
solution.zh: SWE-Review是一个通过引入智能代理代码审查来闭环问题解决的框架。给定一个问题描述和一个AI生成的PR，审查代理在可执行环境中主动探索仓库——读取文件、跟踪调用链、检查测试结果——然后做出二元决策（批准或请求更改），并生成结构化的、局部的修订反馈。这与单轮固定上下文审查有本质不同，后者仅使用静态差异或周围代码，对于跨多个文件或模块的非局部错误而言是脆弱的。关键洞察在于，智能代理审查员可以根据其发现的证据调整其信息收集过程，从而能够将症状追溯到上游根本原因。这产生了更准确的决策和更可操作的反馈，实现了迭代改进PR质量的生成-审查-修订循环，直到审查员批准，从而从开环生成转向闭环解决。

methodology.en: SWE-Review operates as an iterative generate-review-revise loop. Initially, an AI coding agent (e.g., GLM-5, Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B, Qwen3-30B-A3B) generates a candidate PR from a real software issue. This PR, along with the issue text and the full repository, is passed to an agentic reviewer model (Claude Opus 4.6 for evaluation, or a trained 8B model for scalable settings). The reviewer is not limited to static context; it can perform exploration actions in an execution environment, such as reading arbitrary files, searching code, following import/call chains, and running tests. After a configurable number of steps, the reviewer outputs a binary decision (approved or request changes) and a structured diagnosis specifying needed changes. If the decision is 'request changes', the PR is returned to the coding agent along with the feedback, and the coding agent revises accordingly. The cycle repeats until approval or a maximum iteration limit. For evaluation, the authors propose SWE-Review-Bench, a benchmark of 1,384 instances from SWE-bench Verified, each with an issue, a generated PR from one of three models, and a ground-truth label (derived from a held-out reference). The dataset includes a difficulty stratification (easy, medium, hard) based on post-hoc analysis. Metrics are Decision Accuracy (DA)—matching the ground-truth accept/reject decision—and Resolve Rate after Revision (RRR)—proportion of rejected PRs that, after revision guided by the review, pass the task's unit tests. Additionally, SWE-Review-Traj is curated: a dataset of agentic reviewer trajectories used to train open reviewer models (e.g., 8B LLMs) and study test-time scaling. The comparison baselines are single-turn fixed-context reviews: diff-only (reviewer sees only the patch) and diff+context (patch plus issue and changed-file snippets). Training of reviewer models uses behavioral cloning on SWE-Review-Traj trajectories.
methodology.zh: SWE-Review作为一个迭代的生成-审查-修订循环运行。最初，一个AI编码代理（例如，GLM-5、Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B、Qwen3-30B-A3B）从一个真实软件问题生成一个候选PR。该PR连同问题文本和完整仓库被传递给一个智能代理审查模型（用于评估的Claude Opus 4.6，或用于可扩展设置的一个经过训练的8B模型）。审查员不限于静态上下文；它可以在执行环境中执行探索动作，如读取任意文件、搜索代码、跟踪导入/调用链以及运行测试。在可配置的步骤数后，审查员输出一个二元决策（批准或请求更改）和一个指定所需更改的结构化诊断。如果决策是‘请求更改’，则PR连同反馈返回给编码代理，编码代理相应地修改。循环重复直到批准或达到最大迭代次数。为了评估，作者提出了SWE-Review-Bench，一个包含来自SWE-bench Verified的1,384个实例的基准，每个实例包括一个问题、一个来自三个模型之一的生成PR和一个真实标签（来自一个保留参考）。数据集包括基于事后分析的难度分层（简单、中等、困难）。指标是决策准确性（DA）——匹配真实接受/拒绝决策——和修订后解决率（RRR）——在审查指导下修订后通过任务单元测试的被拒绝PR的比例。此外，还整理了SWE-Review-Traj：一个智能代理审查员轨迹数据集，用于训练开放审查模型（例如，8B LLMs）和研究测试时扩展。比较基线是单轮固定上下文审查：仅差异（审查员仅看到补丁）和差异+上下文（补丁加上问题和更改文件片段）。审查员模型的训练使用SWE-Review-Traj轨迹的行为克隆。

experiments.en: Experiments are conducted on SWE-Review-Bench with PRs generated by three models: GLM-5, Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B, and Qwen3-30B-A3B. The reviewer (Claude Opus 4.6) is fixed across settings. Agentic review outperforms both single-turn baselines on all splits. For the Qwen3-30B-A3B split, agentic review achieves approximately 75% DA vs. 65% for single-turn, and RRR of ~56% vs. ~45% (Figure 3). On SWE-bench Verified, iterative review-guided revision raises the resolve rate from 27.5% to 56.9% (Qwen3-30B-A3B), from 50.9% to 68.8% (Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B), and from 72.2% to 75.4% (GLM-5) (Figure 1). Gains are largest on hard instances (Figure 4). For test-time scaling, a trained 8B reviewer is compared against a verifier (model scoring candidate PRs) and a critic (model providing scalar feedback), using Qwen3-30B-A3B as the coding agent. Review-guided iterative revision achieves 38.4% resolve rate after 4 rounds (starting from 22.9%), using far fewer samples than best-of-N sampling with verifier (Figure 5). With the stronger Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B agent, the reviewer reaches 48.1% at K=16 vs. 44.4% for verifier, while using only 6.0 samples on average (a 62.5% reduction) (Figure 7). Completion rates are above 95% across all settings.
experiments.zh: 实验在SWE-Review-Bench上进行，使用由三个模型生成的PR：GLM-5、Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B和Qwen3-30B-A3B。审查员（Claude Opus 4.6）在所有设置中固定。智能代理审查在所有分割上都优于两个单轮基线。对于Qwen3-30B-A3B分割，智能代理审查达到了约75%的DA，而单轮为65%，RRR约为56%对45%（图3）。在SWE-bench Verified上，迭代审查指导的修订将解决率从27.5%提高到56.9%（Qwen3-30B-A3B），从50.9%提高到68.8%（Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B），从72.2%提高到75.4%（GLM-5）（图1）。在困难实例上增益最大（图4）。对于测试时扩展，将一个经过训练的8B审查员与一个验证器（对候选PR打分的模型）和一个批评家（提供标量反馈的模型）进行比较，使用Qwen3-30B-A3B作为编码代理。审查指导的迭代修订在4轮后达到38.4%的解决率（从22.9%开始），使用的样本远少于带有验证器的最佳N采样（图5）。使用更强的Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B代理，审查员在K=16时达到48.1%，而验证器为44.4%，同时平均仅使用6.0个样本（减少62.5%）（图7）。所有设置的完成率都在95%以上。

conclusion.en: The paper finds that an agentic generate-review-revise loop significantly enhances AI patch quality by iteratively filtering out overfitted solutions through contextual repository exploration. This closed-loop method consistently achieves higher resolve rates and produces more useful revisions than one-shot generation or single-turn review, demonstrating a strong link between review accuracy and repair success. For researchers aiming to build reliable automated program repair systems, the framework provides a practical path toward closed-loop issue resolution that efficiently scales with test-time compute. However, its reliance on multi-step exploration and execution environments means current effectiveness is primarily applicable to tasks with accessible validation infrastructure.
conclusion.zh: 论文发现，通过上下文仓库探索迭代过滤过拟合的解决方案，智能代理生成-审查-修订循环显著提高了AI补丁质量。这种闭环方法持续实现了比一次性生成或单轮审查更高的解决率并产生更有用的修订，展示了审查准确性与修复成功之间的强关联。对于旨在构建可靠的自动程序修复系统的研究人员，该框架为闭环问题解决提供了一条实用路径，该路径能随着测试时计算高效扩展。然而，它对多步探索和执行环境的依赖意味着当前的有效性主要适用于具有可访问验证基础设施的任务。

question.en: How does the iterative generate-review-revise loop in SWE-Review reduce the risk of overfitting (plausible but incorrect patches) compared to one-shot PR generation?
question.zh: 与一次性PR生成相比，SWE-Review中的迭代生成-审查-修订循环如何降低过拟合（看似合理但不正确的补丁）的风险？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in avoiding overfitting by evaluating whether the review and revision process catches and corrects plausible but erroneous patches.
reason.zh: 通过评估审查和修订过程是否捕获并纠正看似合理但错误的补丁，直接回应用户对避免过拟合的兴趣。

question.en: In what ways does SWE-Review's agentic reviewer perform patch validation using repository exploration and test execution, and how does this approach compare to traditional APR validation methods like test suite execution alone?
question.zh: SWE-Review的智能代理审查员通过仓库探索和测试执行执行补丁验证的方式有哪些？这种方法与传统的APR验证方法（如仅执行测试套件）相比如何？

reason.en: Connects to the user's focus on patch validation and testing, exploring how the reviewer's broader context (e.g., following call chains) improves validation beyond simple test reuse.
reason.zh: 联系用户对补丁验证和测试的关注，探讨审查员的更广泛上下文（例如，跟踪调用链）如何超越简单的测试复用来改进验证。

question.en: What does SWE-Review-Bench reveal about the relationship between review correctness and downstream revision usefulness, and how does this inform the design of APR systems that aim to produce reliable patches?
question.zh: SWE-Review-Bench揭示了审查正确性与下游修订有用性之间的什么关系？这对旨在产生可靠补丁的APR系统设计有何启示？

reason.en: Leverages the benchmark release to provide empirical insights into patch correctness and improvement, aligning with the user's interest in empirical studies and benchmark evaluation.
reason.zh: 利用基准测试发布提供关于补丁正确性和改进的实证洞察，与用户对实证研究和基准评估的兴趣一致。

question.en: How could the agentic review framework be extended to incorporate explicit fault localization feedback, and what are the current limitations of the reviewer in diagnosing the root cause of a failed patch?
question.zh: 如何扩展智能代理审查框架以纳入显式的故障定位反馈？审查员在诊断失败补丁的根本原因方面目前存在哪些局限性？

reason.en: Addresses the user's interest in fault localization by examining whether the reviewer's feedback identifies specific fault locations or merely provides general revision suggestions.
reason.zh: 通过检查审查员的反馈是否识别特定故障位置或仅提供一般修订建议，回应用户对故障定位的兴趣。

question.en: How does the iterative generate-review-revise loop in SWE-Review reduce the risk of overfitting (plausible but incorrect patches) compared to one-shot PR generation?
question.zh: 与一次性PR生成相比，SWE-Review中的迭代生成-审查-修订循环如何降低过拟合（看似合理但不正确的补丁）的风险？

reason.en: Directly addresses the user's interest in avoiding overfitting by evaluating whether the review and revision process catches and corrects plausible but erroneous patches.
reason.zh: 通过评估审查和修订过程是否捕获并纠正看似合理但错误的补丁，直接回应用户对避免过拟合的兴趣。

answer.en: The iterative generate-review-revise loop in SWE-Review reduces the risk of overfitting by incorporating a structured review phase that detects and corrects plausible but incorrect patches. The abstract states that a 'reviewer agent explores the repository, decides whether the PR should be accepted, and provides structured feedback for revision,' directly targeting erroneous patches. Experiments further show that this loop 'continuously improves PRs through a generate-review-revise loop, outperforms single-turn fixed-context review in both decision accuracy and resolve rate after revision,' indicating that iterative validation effectively filters out overfitted solutions compared to one-shot generation.
answer.zh: SWE-Review中的迭代生成-审查-修订循环通过纳入结构化的审查阶段来检测和纠正看似合理但不正确的补丁，从而降低过拟合的风险。摘要指出，‘审查代理探索仓库，决定是否应接受PR，并提供结构化的修订反馈’，直接针对错误的补丁。实验进一步表明，该循环‘通过生成-审查-修订循环持续改进PR，在决策准确性和修订后解决率方面均优于单轮固定上下文审查’，表明迭代验证有效过滤了相比一次性生成更容易过拟合的解决方案。

question.en: In what ways does SWE-Review's agentic reviewer perform patch validation using repository exploration and test execution, and how does this approach compare to traditional APR validation methods like test suite execution alone?
question.zh: SWE-Review的智能代理审查员通过仓库探索和测试执行执行补丁验证的方式有哪些？这种方法与传统的APR验证方法（如仅执行测试套件）相比如何？

reason.en: Connects to the user's focus on patch validation and testing, exploring how the reviewer's broader context (e.g., following call chains) improves validation beyond simple test reuse.
reason.zh: 联系用户对补丁验证和测试的关注，探讨审查员的更广泛上下文（例如，跟踪调用链）如何超越简单的测试复用来改进验证。

answer.en: The paper's abstract states that the agentic reviewer "explores the repository" to gather context and decide on PR acceptance, but it does not explicitly describe test execution or compare to traditional APR validation like test suite execution alone. The only performance comparison mentioned is that agentic review "outperforms single-turn fixed-context review in both decision accuracy and resolve rate after revision," which does not directly address test-based validation methods. Therefore, the provided text lacks specific evidence on how repository exploration and test execution are performed or how they compare to traditional test suite validation.
answer.zh: 论文摘要指出，智能代理审查员‘探索仓库’以收集上下文并决定PR的接受，但没有明确描述测试执行或与仅执行测试套件的传统APR验证进行比较。唯一提到的性能比较是智能代理审查‘在决策准确性和修订后解决率方面均优于单轮固定上下文审查’，这并未直接涉及基于测试的验证方法。因此，提供的文本缺乏关于仓库探索和测试执行如何执行，或它们与传统测试套件验证相比的具体证据。

question.en: What does SWE-Review-Bench reveal about the relationship between review correctness and downstream revision usefulness, and how does this inform the design of APR systems that aim to produce reliable patches?
question.zh: SWE-Review-Bench揭示了审查正确性与下游修订有用性之间的什么关系？这对旨在产生可靠补丁的APR系统设计有何启示？

reason.en: Leverages the benchmark release to provide empirical insights into patch correctness and improvement, aligning with the user's interest in empirical studies and benchmark evaluation.
reason.zh: 利用基准测试发布提供关于补丁正确性和改进的实证洞察，与用户对实证研究和基准评估的兴趣一致。

answer.en: SWE-Review-Bench reveals a positive relationship between review correctness (measured by decision accuracy) and downstream revision usefulness (measured by resolve rate after revision). The paper finds that agentic review "outperforms single-turn fixed-context review in both decision accuracy and resolve rate after revision," indicating that improvements in review correctness directly translate into more useful revisions. This informs APR system design that to produce reliable patches, systems should adopt a closed-loop generate-review-revise cycle, as it "continuously improves PRs" and enhances both the accuracy of patch evaluation and the likelihood of successful repair.
answer.zh: SWE-Review-Bench揭示了审查正确性（通过决策准确性衡量）与下游修订有用性（通过修订后解决率衡量）之间的正向关系。论文发现智能代理审查‘在决策准确性和修订后解决率方面均优于单轮固定上下文审查’，表明审查正确性的提高直接转化为更有用的修订。这启示APR系统设计：要产生可靠的补丁，系统应采用闭环的生成-审查-修订循环，因为它‘持续改进PR’，并同时提高补丁评估的准确性和成功修复的可能性。

question.en: How could the agentic review framework be extended to incorporate explicit fault localization feedback, and what are the current limitations of the reviewer in diagnosing the root cause of a failed patch?
question.zh: 如何扩展智能代理审查框架以纳入显式的故障定位反馈？审查员在诊断失败补丁的根本原因方面目前存在哪些局限性？

reason.en: Addresses the user's interest in fault localization by examining whether the reviewer's feedback identifies specific fault locations or merely provides general revision suggestions.
reason.zh: 通过检查审查员的反馈是否识别特定故障位置或仅提供一般修订建议，回应用户对故障定位的兴趣。

answer.en: The paper does not explicitly discuss extending the framework for explicit fault localization feedback or detail limitations in root cause diagnosis. It describes the reviewer agent as providing 'structured feedback for revision' but does not specify whether this feedback identifies specific fault locations or is general. Consequently, the current limitations of the reviewer in diagnosing root causes are not addressed in the provided content. To incorporate explicit fault localization, one would likely need to augment the reviewer's feedback to include precise code locations, but this is not covered by the paper.
answer.zh: 论文没有明确讨论扩展框架以提供显式故障定位反馈，也没有详细说明根本原因诊断的局限性。它将审查代理描述为提供‘结构化的修订反馈’，但没有说明这些反馈是指定具体故障位置还是一般性的。因此，所提供的文本没有涉及审查员在诊断根本原因方面的当前局限性。要纳入显式故障定位，可能需要增强审查员的反馈以包括精确的代码位置，但论文未涉及这一点。
